ops.body: 2026-07-19 W2 diagnosis + user decision. The mirror-mode accrual stall is DIAGNOSED (mixed): (1) v002's operating points don't transfer to live traffic — the gate's corpus-fitted budget_2_per_100 threshold 0.9985 exceeds the highest gate score ever seen live (0.9945; 0/51 rows reach it) and the v002 drafter's abstain threshold collapsed to 0.0001 (v001: 0.2373), so the fitted 2-fires/100-turns budget delivers 0/100 live; (2) a now-fixed mlx-extra crash loop had the daemon down ~07-17→07-18 17:55 (791 KeepAlive thrashes; 0.16.0's version-pinned plist fixed it); (3) NOT caps, NOT a stale wheel, NOT a reporting artifact (proposals cratered after 07-14: 24/8/1/1/0/0 per-day). Stage read: no_go on live_allow — allow_projects=[] makes the flip a no-op, the v001-era deliverable evidence is 0 helpful / 2 nuisance (17/18 fires are one identical hallucinated draft from the empty-context era), clean v002 has ZERO mirror data, and 15/18 fires carry project="" (could never deliver). USER DECISION: observability + re-fit first — no interim threshold overrides; land per-moment score persistence (every scored moment durably rowed incl. gate-suppressed) + the empty-project fix in the next release, collect the live score distribution, re-fit both budget thresholds so the gate actually fires ~2/100 live, re-promote, THEN accrue a v002-only mirror window and bring the allow_projects decision. Implementation lane in flight (wf_fcb77baf, w3 workspace). Also landed: the 21st-steer budget-exhaustion test (3af2de9f, 5 parameterized cases incl. per-day reset) — the criterion's budget-exhaustion clause is code-covered; the live drill happens at flip time.
